article_title: DOE Awards $94 Million to Eight Companies to Accelerate Light-Water SMR Deployment and Build U.S. Nuclear Supply Chain
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

content_preview.title: DOE Awards $94 Million to Eight Companies to Accelerate Light-Water SMR Deployment and Build U.S. Nuclear Supply Chain
content_preview.summary: The Department of Energy selected eight U.S. companies for $94 million in Tier 2 awards targeting site permitting and domestic supply chain gaps for Gen III+ small modular reactors.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The U.S. Department of Energy announced on May 14, 2026, that it had selected eight American companies to receive more than $94 million in federal cost-shared funding to accelerate the deployment of advanced light-water small modular reactors (SMRs). The awards, made under the Tier 2 Fast Follower Deployment Support component of the [Generation III+ SMR Pathway to Deployment Program](https://www.energy.gov/ne/generation-iii-small-modular-reactor-program), target three bottlenecks th...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News article with a clear four-section format (Overview, What We Know, What We Don't Know, Analysis). Appropriate technical depth for the nuclear energy beat. Award amounts and company names verified against the primary DOE press release. The article accurately follows the primary source (DOE press release, source-0) throughout.
editor_notes.source_verification: Source-0 (DOE press release, energy.gov, source-0.html.gz): VERIFIED. All eight award recipients, dollar amounts, locations, and the Chris Wright quote confirmed verbatim. Primary source for all core claims. Source-1 (DOE Gen III+ SMR program page, energy.gov, source-1.html.gz): VERIFIED. Confirms Tier 1 and Tier 2 program structure, $900 million total, $800M Tier 1 and ~$100M Tier 2 split. Notes the Tier 1 announcement as 'November 2025' while source-0 says 'December 2025' — internal DOE discrepancy; the article follows source-1's 'November 2025' phrasing, which is consistent with what the DOE program page states. Source-2 (Rigzone, source-2.html.gz): UNVERIFIABLE — snapshot returned only a JavaScript bot-challenge page (202 status, 1979 bytes). The specific claims attributed to Rigzone in the article (Framatome fuel capacity expansion, GNF fuel rod production line) are independently confirmed by source-0 and source-3. The Rigzone citation is supplementary. Source-3 (ANS Nuclear Newswire, source-3.html.gz): VERIFIED. Confirms all eight award recipients, amounts, and locations. ANS is the professional publication of the American Nuclear Society — a credible specialized trade outlet. Independently confirms Framatome's 200 metric tons of uranium annual capacity expansion in Richland, WA.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All award amounts and recipient names match source-0. The Chris Wright quote ('President Trump has made clear that America is going to build more energy, not less...') is verbatim from source-0. One naming discrepancy: the article uses 'American Forgemasters Company' which matches source-0 (DOE press release) exactly; sources 1 and 3 use 'North American Forgemasters Company'. The article correctly followed its primary source. The Tier 1 award timing is stated as 'November 2025' by source-1; source-0 says 'December 2025'. The article's 'November 2025' claim aligns with the DOE program page (source-1) but contradicts the DOE press release (source-0). This is an internal DOE inconsistency rather than a fabricated claim. The DOE program page (source-1) is more authoritative for historical dates within the program itself.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and factual throughout. No editorializing or sensationalism. The Analysis section appropriately contextualizes the awards within the broader U.S. nuclear revival without promotional language. The reference to domestic nuclear manufacturing 'atrophied since the 1970s and 1980s' is a factually grounded observation, not opinion.
editor_notes.originality: No duplicate coverage. The only related existing article is the May 3 OPG Darlington foundation pour (referenced appropriately in the Analysis section as context). This DOE Tier 2 award announcement is distinct and covers new information.
concerns: Rigzone (source-2) snapshot is a bot-challenge page — the source is not verifiable from the snapshot. However, all claims attributed to Rigzone are independently confirmed by sources 0 and 3, so this does not affect factual accuracy.

rigzone.com is not on the source allowlist. Given that Rigzone is widely used in energy journalism and the claims it is cited for are independently supported by primary sources, this is a minor concern.
recommendations: Consider adding rigzone.com to the source allowlist, as it is a credible energy industry trade outlet cited here only for supplementary context already confirmed by primary sources.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Solid, well-researched News article drawing on primary DOE sources. All material claims are verified against the DOE press release. The Rigzone source cannot be verified from the snapshot but is only cited for supplementary detail confirmed elsewhere. The automated warning about rigzone.com not being in the allowlist is valid. Verdict: APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS — the article publishes with a corrections record noting the Rigzone allowlist status and the Tier 1 date discrepancy between DOE sources.
